'''Sheyvan''' was a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Assassin/Legends|assassin]] who, in the early [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], served as one of the [[Emperor's Hand/Legends|Emperor's Hands]]. He was assigned to train the [[Cloning/Legends|cloned]] [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] of the deceased [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Sa Cuis]] in the techniques of [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] and [[The Force/Legends|Force]] combat.

==Biography==
Sheyvan eventually discovered that he was not the only Hand in the service of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], as he had been led to believe. He was bitter about it, but he remained loyal to the Emperor and [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], at least until the right opportunity would show itself.

On the planet [[Yinchorr/Legends|Yinchorr]], Sheyvan showed a demonstration of his stormtroopers to the Emperor, Vader, and [[Erv Lekauf]]. After they proved successful against the clones of Lekauf, the Emperor ordered Sheyvan and the clones to return with him, Vader, and Lekauf to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. He planned to train the Cuis clones further and incorporate them into the [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]].

En route to the capital, about half-way into the journey, Sheyvan and his troopers suddenly hijacked the [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|''Lambda''-class shuttle]] and planned to kill the Emperor. Darth Vader, Lekauf, and Lekauf's clones [[Skirmish aboard ST 321|fought]] their way through to the hatch that Sheyvan and his men sealed off. In a tense battle, Vader killed all of the Sa Cuis clones while Lekauf was left injured with severe burns from a [[Flamethrower/Legends|flamethrower]]. Sheyvan explained to Vader that the Emperor was using everyone and asked him to help overthrow the elder [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] Lord. Vader, though tempted to claim [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] for himself, refused and after a quick [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] duel, Sheyvan was killed. However, despite holding back fire and surviving smoke, Sheyvan was a still formidable fighter, and Vader admired this. He genuinely regretted the final stroke that sliced Sheyvan shoulder to hip and left him for dead on the deck.
